content: In order to make serotonin inside your brain, which protects you from stress, you need to get tryptophan into your brain.
Tryptophan comes from the protein you eat, and it competes with other amino acids for entry into the brain.
Insulin brings other competing amino acids into muscle cells, and it is mainly produced from eating high-glycemic carbs.
High-glycemic carbs, such as white rice, cause a larger insulin spike and increase tryptophan entry into the brain.
Eating a high-carb meal in the morning can help maintain serotonin levels at night, aiding sleep and stress resistance.
Natural, whole foods like potatoes and sweet potatoes are good sources of high-glycemic carbs for boosting serotonin.
If on a low-carb diet, tryptophan supplements taken on an empty stomach might help increase serotonin levels.
